findings.description: Azure IoT Hub 'iothub-ot-platform' does not have Microsoft Defender for IoT enabled. Over 3,400 OT devices (PLCs, sensors, actuators) connected to this hub are not monitored for anomalous behavior, unauthorized commands, or firmware tampering. IEC 62443 SL-2 requires continuous monitoring of OT assets.
findings.remediation: Enable Microsoft Defender for IoT. Deploy OT network sensors in passive monitoring mode at plant-alpha, plant-beta, and plant-gamma sites. Configure alerts for: new device connections, anomalous traffic patterns, Modbus/OPC-UA command anomalies.

findings.description: Plant Alpha control network (Zone 2 — Process Control) has a direct routed connection to the enterprise IT network (Zone 3) without a DMZ or unidirectional gateway (data diode). IEC 62443 requires segmentation between Level 3 (Enterprise) and Level 2 (Control). A malware infection in the IT network can laterally move directly to PLC/SCADA systems.
findings.remediation: Implement network DMZ between IT and OT zones. Deploy Waterfall Security Unidirectional Security Gateway or equivalent data diode for north-bound telemetry. Remove all direct IT-to-OT routed connections. IEC 62443-3-3 requires conduit design for zone-crossing traffic.

findings.description: IoT Hub has per-device SAS key authentication disabled and uses a shared device key for all devices in the 'plant-sensors' device group. Compromise of any single edge device exposes the shared key, allowing an attacker to impersonate any device and inject false sensor readings into the process control system.
findings.remediation: Migrate all OT devices to X.509 certificate-based authentication with per-device certificates issued by the on-premise CA. Revoke the shared SAS key. For legacy devices that cannot support X.509, use per-device SAS keys with 30-day rotation.

findings.description: Azure Time Series Insights environment 'tsi-plant-telemetry' has public network access enabled. The OT process telemetry (temperature, pressure, flow rates, valve positions) is accessible from the internet to anyone with a valid SAS token. Operational data reveals production process parameters sensitive to industrial espionage.
findings.remediation: Restrict Time Series Insights to private endpoint only. Disable public network access. Use Azure Private Link to access TSI from the plant analytics subnet only. Rotate all existing SAS tokens.

findings.description: Siemens S7-1500 PLC 'plc-reactor-control-01' at Plant Beta reactor zone retains factory default credentials (admin/admin). Microsoft Defender for IoT passive scan detected default credential patterns in TIA Portal v16 traffic. This PLC controls the reactor temperature setpoint — unauthorized commands could cause safety incidents.
findings.remediation: Change PLC default credentials immediately using TIA Portal. Implement PLC access control: separate operator and engineer roles. Enable Siemens Security Integrated audit logging. Change password quarterly per IEC 62443 SL-2 requirements.

findings.description: Azure Blob Storage account 'stiotfirmware001' storing OT device firmware images does not have Azure Defender for Storage enabled, and firmware files are stored without code-signing verification metadata. An attacker who gains write access can replace firmware with a malicious version that will be deployed to production OT devices.
findings.remediation: Enable Microsoft Defender for Storage on the firmware account. Implement firmware code signing: sign all firmware with the corporate PKI, verify signatures before deployment in edge-gateway firmware_updater.py. Enable immutable blob storage with time-based retention.

findings.description: ExpressRoute circuit 'er-plant-to-azure' connecting plant sites to Azure does not have MACsec (Layer 2 encryption) enabled. OT telemetry data travels over the carrier network between plant and Azure without encryption at the physical/data link layer. Carrier employees or tapping at the co-location facility can intercept OT data.
findings.remediation: Enable MACsec encryption on the ExpressRoute Direct port. Use AES-256-GCM cipher suite. Configure MACsec Key Agreement (MKA) with pre-shared keys stored in Key Vault.

findings.description: SCADA HMI 'hmi-scada-main' at Plant Gamma runs Windows 7 Embedded (end-of-life January 2020). The HMI has direct network access to Zone 2 control systems and is used by plant operators to interface with the DCS. EOL OS has unpatched vulnerabilities including EternalBlue (MS17-010) which could allow lateral movement to PLCs.
findings.remediation: Upgrade HMI OS to Windows 10 IoT Enterprise LTSC or Windows Server 2022. If upgrade is not immediately feasible: isolate HMI to its own VLAN with strict ACLs, disable network shares, deploy application whitelisting (Windows Defender Application Control), and monitor for anomalous network connections.

findings.description: Log Analytics workspace 'law-iot-security' has 2-year retention configured for OT security events. This exceeds the NERC CIP-008 minimum 3-year requirement (note: Azure Archive tier used for months 13-24). Defender for IoT alerts are forwarded to this workspace.

IoT Hub event routing to Event Hub uses the default $Default consumer group shared across the Python analytics service, the Azure Stream Analytics job, and the alert engine. Consumer group contention causes message processing delays. More critically, a bug in one consumer can cause checkpoint misalignment affecting all consumers including safety alert processing.
findings.remediation: Create separate consumer groups for each subscriber: 'cg-analytics', 'cg-stream-analytics', 'cg-alerts'. Update each service to use its dedicated consumer group. This also improves resilience — one slow consumer cannot block others.
